Stages of chronic kidney disease (CKD).description: CKD is classified by severity into stages 1–5. N18.2 = mild CKD (stage 2); N18.30–N18.32 = moderate CKD (stage 3); N18.4 = severe CKD (stage 4); N18.6 = ESRD.
Stages of chronic kidney disease (CKD).ESRD rule: If both a stage and ESRD are documented, assign only N18.6.

Chronic kidney disease and kidney transp.description: Post-kidney transplant patients may still have CKD due to incomplete restoration of kidney function. CKD alone is not a transplant complication.
Chronic kidney disease and kidney transp.coding: Assign appropriate N18 code for CKD stage and Z94.0 for kidney transplant status. For complications such as failure/rejection, see Section I.C.19.g.
Chronic kidney disease and kidney transp.documentation_unclear: Query provider if unclear whether CKD is due to a transplant complication.

Chronic kidney disease with other condit.description: CKD often coexists with serious conditions like diabetes mellitus and hypertension.
Chronic kidney disease with other condit.sequencing: Sequence CKD code in relation to other conditions based on Tabular List conventions.
references: See Section I.C.9: Hypertensive chronic kidney disease.
See Section I.C.19: Chronic kidney disease and kidney transplant complications.
